title: homalium planiflorum subsp. planiflorum
intro: Homalium planiflorum subsp. planiflorum, also known as African dogwood, is a plant species in the family Salicaceae. It is traditionally used in various parts of the world for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating various infections. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared in various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments.
